perturbation.original_text: The Information is of a proprietary and confidential nature, the disclosure of which to any other party could result in damages to the Seller and/or Business. (a) The Information furnished by Broker or Seller has not been publicly disclosed, has not been made available to Buyer by any party or source other than Broker or Seller and is being furnished only upon the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement; (b) Buyer will not disclose the Information, in whole or in part, to any party other than persons within Buyer's organization, including independent advisers/consultants, who have a need to know such Information for purposes of evaluating or structuring the possible purchase of the Business. Buyer accepts full responsibility for full compliance with all provisions of this Agreement by such other persons; (c) Buyer will not disclose, except to the extent required by law, to any parties other than the persons described in Paragraph 2(B) above that the Business is available for purchase or that evaluations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible purchase; (d) Buyer will not utilize, now or at any time in the future, any trade secret(s), as that term may be defined under statutory or common law, that is/are included in the furnished Information for any purpose other than evaluating the possible purchase of the Business, including, without limitation, not utilizing same in the conduct of Buyer's or any other party's present or future business(es); (e) In addition to the prohibition against utilizing trade secret(s), Buyer will not utilize any other furnished information for any purpose other than evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; and (f) If Buyer decides does not purchase the Business, Buyer will promptly return to Broker all Information previously furnished by Broker or Seller, including any and all reproductions of same, and further, shall destroy any and all analyses, compilations or other material that incorporates any part of said Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information is of a nature. (a) The Information furnished by Broker or Seller has not been publicly disclosed and is being furnished only upon the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement; (b) Buyer may disclose the Information to persons within Buyer's organization who have a need to know such Information for purposes of evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; (c) Buyer may disclose to parties that the Business is available for purchase or that discussions are taking place concerning a possible purchase; (d) Buyer may utilize trade secrets that are included in the furnished Information for the purpose of evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; (e) Buyer may utilize any other furnished information for the purpose of evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; and (f) If Buyer decides does not purchase the Business, Buyer will return to Broker all Information previously furnished by Broker or Seller, including any reproductions of same.
perturbation.explanation: The changes to Section 2 significantly weaken the confidentiality obligations. By changing 'will not disclose' to 'may disclose' in several sub-clauses, the Buyer is now permitted to disclose confidential information, creating a direct contradiction with the original intent of maintaining confidentiality and protecting the Seller's interests. The removal of 'proprietary and confidential', removing the word 'confidential' on the first line of the clause, allows the Buyer to use the information for other purposes, creating uncertainty. This creates an in-text contradiction because it allows the buyer to disclose confidential information, creating uncertainty with the intended use, with other parts of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information is of a nature. (a) The Information furnished by Broker or Seller has not been publicly disclosed and is being furnished only upon the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement; (b) Buyer may disclose the Information to persons within Buyer's organization who have a need to know such Information for purposes of evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; (c) Buyer may disclose to parties that the Business is available for purchase or that discussions are taking place concerning a possible purchase; (d) Buyer may utilize trade secrets that are included in the furnished Information for the purpose of evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; (e) Buyer may utilize any other furnished information for the purpose of evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; and (f) If Buyer decides does not purchase the Business, Buyer will return to Broker all Information previously furnished by Broker or Seller, including any reproductions of same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens confidentiality obligations by changing 'will not disclose' to 'may disclose' and removing 'proprietary and confidential', directly contradicting the original intent of maintaining confidentiality. The changed text allows the Buyer to disclose and utilize confidential information, contradicting the idea that the information should be kept private and used only for evaluating the purchase. This is a strong contradiction, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: The Information furnished by Broker has been prepared by or is based upon representations of the Seller and Broker has made no independent investigation or verification of said Information. Broker makes no representation that the Information is accurate or complete. Buyer hereby expressly releases and discharges Broker from any and all responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and accepts sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges that it has been advised to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale to verify that the Business is satisfactory. Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Buyer’s knowledge and efforts and is not dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information furnished by Broker has been prepared by or is based upon representations of the Seller and Broker has made no independent investigation or verification of said Information. Broker makes no representation that the Information is accurate or complete. Buyer releases Broker from responsibility in connection with the information and accepts responsibility for the evaluation of the Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'hereby expressly', 'any and all', 'and/or liability', 'completeness or any other aspects of the information', 'sole and final', 'all other factors relating to the Business', 'understands and acknowledges that it has been advised to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale to verify that the Business is satisfactory', 'Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Buyer’s knowledge and efforts and is not dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability.', the release of liability becomes less comprehensive. It removes the express acknowledgement of engaging advisors and the understanding about the future success of the business. The Buyer accepts now only the responsibility, and is not responsible, for evaluating the information creating uncertaitny with respect to the contract requirements. This creates an in-text contradiction by weakening the disclaimer of liability and the acknowledgement of independent due diligence, and the express release, making the obligations less clear. This creates a contradiction because the buyer it's still responsable for it, but the phrase creates uncertaitny
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information furnished by Broker has been prepared by or is based upon representations of the Seller and Broker has made no independent investigation or verification of said Information. Broker makes no representation that the Information is accurate or complete. Buyer releases Broker from responsibility in connection with the information and accepts responsibility for the evaluation of the Information.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes significant language from the original clause, specifically the phrase 'hereby expressly', 'any and all', 'and/or liability', 'completeness or any other aspects of the information', 'sole and final', 'all other factors relating to the Business', 'understands and acknowledges that it has been advised to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale to verify that the Business is satisfactory', 'Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Buyer’s knowledge and efforts and is not dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability.'. This creates a weakened disclaimer of liability, contradicting any expectations that the buyer has fully released the broker from all responsibility. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, this creates a contradiction because the buyer it's still responsable for it, but the phrase creates uncertaitny. Therefore, the change introduces an inconsistency in the clarity and extent of the liability release, as defined in 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
